items.title: Origin Energy investigating 'potential' customer data breach

children.content: Origin Energy says it does not believe customer credit card or bank details have been affected by a suspected data breach at the company.

articles.title: Origin Energy shares fall as it confirms breach investigation

children.content: Energy retailer Origin says there may have been unauthorised access to customers' personal data. The Australian share market has started its day higher after Wall Street's beaten-down tech and semiconductor stocks rebounded sharply overnight. Follow live.

image.altText: Origin energy img

gallery.altText: Origin's red and white logo is seen on a pile of company reports

items.title: Minister says Australian ISIS-linked 'Doctor Jihad' can rot in Iraqi jail

children.content: Perth paediatrician Tareq Kamleh went to Syria to join the Islamic State group in 2015 and has, until now, been presumed dead.

image.altText: Dr Tareq Kamleh

items.title: Judge to hand down decision in Rebel Wilson defamation case

children.content: Hollywood actor Rebel Wilson will soon hear the decision in the defamation case brought against her by the star of her directorial debut.

image.altText: Rebel Wilson outside court April 30 for defamation trial

items.title: Former Socceroos captain Paul Wade reveals probable CTE diagnosis

children.content: Paul Wade says he wants to share his experience to raise awareness about concussion, repeated head impacts and brain health across all levels of football.

image.altText: Paul Wade

items.title: Lewis breaks Australian 100m record, beats five-time Olympic champ

children.content: Torrie Lewis shows she is hitting her straps ahead of the Commonwealth Games by beating her own record mark during a meet in Slovakia.

image.altText: Torrie Lewis unties her shoelaces while sitting on the track

items.title: How one of Iran's 'Axis' allies is threatening to widen the war

children.content: The Houthi rebels, described as one of Iran's "merchants of chaos", have threatened to widen the conflict in the Middle East by declaring a maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia. Here's what could happen next.

articles.title: Hegseth outlines cost of Iran war at Senate hearing

children.content: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is being questioned on the cost of the war against Iran, his idea of testosterone screening for troops, and the number of service people who have died.

image.altText: Pete Hegseth seated, speaking into a small microphone.

gallery.altText: A satellite image showing a narrow strait of water between two continents.

items.title: Gates Foundation met with Epstein about 30 times, review finds

children.content: Bill Gates was aware of Jeffrey Epstein's 2008 sex offence conviction during their association, an external review of the Gates Foundation finds.

image.altText: Gates walks among a crowd of reports and Capitol Hill staff, giving a small, tight-lipped smile.

items.title: 'We do not want to highlight this yet': AI data centre's FAQ slip-up

children.content: Data centre builder Firmus posted a statement, since removed, indicating its intention to get some of its cooling water from the state-owned irrigation system, adding "but we do not want to highlight this yet".

image.altText: A lot in a residential area with a big building on it.

items.title: Unlawful excavation of river cliff disturbs Aboriginal ancestral remains

children.content: A cliff site in South Australia's Riverland, home to Aboriginal ancestral remains, has been excavated without authorisation.

View of a development site near the river's edge. Houses scattered behind the site.

items.title: Judge says there's a 'real risk' to safety of Bondi Beach attack witnesses

children.content: Twenty witnesses in the Bondi Beach terror attack case have been granted anonymity, with a judge saying there is a "real risk" to their safety.

image.altText: Flowers laid in tribute to bondi victims.

items.title: Boy charged with murdering mother, attempting to kill sister in Perth

children.content: A 16-year-old boy is charged with murdering his mother and attempting to kill his sister in Perth's south-east, as police say the girl is being treated in hospital for "very serious" stab wounds.

image.altText: Police tape off a suburban driveway

items.title: Atlassian marks milestone in $1.4b 'world's tallest hybrid timber' tower in Sydney

children.content: When construction finishes next year, the Sydney building will be the tallest hybrid structure in the world by 100 metres.

items.title: NT enacts 'significant overhaul' of child protection system

children.content: The Northern Territory parliament has passed changes to child protection laws, with the Country Liberal Party describing its amendments as "the most significant overhaul of the Territory's child protection system in a generation".

children.content: The Odyssey's director Sir Christopher Nolan has a stellar catalogue. Interstellar, even. But as his new film smashes box office records, is it safe to crown him the new king of Hollywood?

items.title: Cameron McEvoy has lived a life between trips to Glasgow

items.title: Fox sisters combine in 'dream team' for gold medals at World Championships

items.title: States can use their own emergency warnings when AusAlert goes live

children.content: State and territory representatives had voiced significant concerns about the rollout of the national emergency warning system in a meeting with the minister Kristy McBain.

items.title: Albanese's career mirrors Howard's in two important ways

children.content: Anthony Albanese has been in near-fatal vehicle accidents not once, but three times. Politically, his is also a story of survival against the odds.

items.title: Woman's family wrongly told she was dead in hospital identity error

children.content: A Melbourne hospital apologises after mixing up the medical data of two people, resulting in one of them being wrongly declared dead by police.

image.altText: Michelle Buckley stands indoors, dressed in a warm cardigan.

items.title: How school is different for Jewish Australian kids

children.content: The Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion hears from Jewish school leaders about how they need to implement extraordinary and expensive security measures.

children.content: US investigators say speed was a likely factor in the crash that killed actor Kaylee Hottle, 18.

items.title: AI companies 'stole' Australian culture, news bosses say

children.content: The heads of the ABC, SBS, Nine and News Corp say AI companies should strike licensing deals rather than freely train on Australian news, warning journalism is a public good that must be protected.

image.altText: Hamish MacDonald,  Allesandra Galloni, Hugh Marks, Michael Miller, Jane Palfreyman, Matt Stanton.

items.title: Family's cross-contamination nightmare after contractors spread mould

children.content: A Queensland family is living in an overcrowded caravan and shed after insurance provider Suncorp exacerbated damage in their home, initially caused by a leak.

items.title: Zelenskyy names new Ukrainian commander-in-chief

children.content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy replaces the country's commander-in-chief after protests against his sacking of the defence minister last week.

items.title: France approves ban on social media for children under 15

children.content: French lawmakers have approved a ban on social media access for children under the age of 15

items.title: Map reveals Australia's 'pharmacy deserts' are not all in the bush

children.content: Australia's "pharmacy deserts" have been mapped for the first time, revealing remote NT communities and outer-city Melbourne have fewer pharmacies per person than some other parts of the country.

items.title: WA man dies after not being able to get antipsychotic medication

children.content: Bradley Buswell, aged in his 40s and living with schizophrenia, died in state care after his medication became unavailable due to national shortages. An ongoing coronial inquest is probing the circumstances of his death.

image.altText: Bradley Buswell smiles wearing a collared shirt.

items.title: Spanish chief accuses FIFA boss of 'destroying the football industry'

children.content: FIFA president Gianni Infantino comes in for more heavy criticism, this time at the hands of La Liga boss Javier Tebas.

image.altText: Gianni Infantino holds the World Cup trophy with Donald Trump

items.title: Son's dying wish to see mum play soccer now helps her cope with loss

children.content: After Spencer Farley was diagnosed with terminal cancer, he asked his mum to start playing soccer. In the 12 months since his death, the game has kept her going.
